title: Image Distribution and Upgrade Progress Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Visibility into in-flight and completed image upgrades shows that the managed network is being moved toward the approved software baseline in a controlled way.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides evidence of software distribution and upgrade progress for managed infrastructure aligned to the change-approved golden image process.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard or saved search for UC-5.13.57 with counts of upgradeStatus by family during an approved change window; tickets linking to the CAB record.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracking success and failure of firmware or image rollouts is an input to timely flaw remediation and patch/firmware program execution.
compliance.controlObjective: Surfaces which devices remain in IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, or long-running scheduled states so remediation can be prioritized during upgrade campaigns.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Weekly or per-change report of SUCCESS vs FAILED counts by deviceFamily from the SWIM data in Splunk, stored with the change record.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`. via custom scripted input polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance/detail?complianceType=IMAGE` (deploy per docs/guides/catalyst-center.md § Custom Scripted Inputs). Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `imageCompliance` (string), `runningVersion` (string), `targetVersion` (string), `deviceFamily` (string), `deviceName` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `upgradeStatus` (string). CIM data model: Change.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input for SWIM API
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" upgradeStatus=* | stats count by upgradeStatus, deviceFamily | eval status_order=case(upgradeStatus="SUCCESS",1,upgradeStatus="IN_PROGRESS",2,upgradeStatus="SCHEDULED",3,upgradeStatus="FAILED",4,1==1,5) | sort status_order -count
description: Tracks the progress of firmware distribution and upgrade operations across the managed device fleet, showing success, in-progress, scheduled, and failed states.
value: Large-scale firmware upgrades are high-risk operations. Real-time progress tracking ensures failures are caught immediately and upgrade windows are respected.
implementation:
Ingest `upgradeStatus` (and related metadata) from Catalyst Center SWIM into `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim` using the same poller as UC-5.13.55. Primary Intent API: `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` (often returns schedule, state, and device identifiers; field names may include versions of SUCCESS, IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, etc.). Re-authenticate on each run or cache the token with expiry handling via `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token`.

**Scripted input example** (`interval` aligned to your change window, for example 300–900 seconds during upgrades):

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_swim/bin/collect_swim.py]
interval = 300
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:swim
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```

**HEC / event notifications (optional):** For faster state transitions, register Catalyst Center event notifications (if your release exposes SWIM task events) to Splunk HEC and merge into the same sourcetype or a secondary `cisco:dnac:swim:event` that you `union` in dashboards.
visualization: Stacked bar or column chart of count by `upgradeStatus` and `deviceFamily`, single values for in-progress and failed, timeline if `_time` from event notifications is used.
knownFalsePositives:
**Large upgrade window with many devices in IN_PROGRESS or SCHEDULED status.** During a major firmware campaign, hundreds of devices may be in various upgrade states simultaneously. The progress dashboard may appear alarming if the viewer is unaware of the planned campaign. Distinguish by correlating with ITSM change records for approved upgrade campaigns. Suppress by annotating the progress tracker with the approved upgrade window.

**Upgrade progress stalling due to Catalyst Center resource constraints.** Catalyst Center has concurrent upgrade limits. Devices in SCHEDULED status may wait longer than expected when many devices are queued. The progress tracker may show a plateau. Distinguish by checking whether `upgradeStatus=SCHEDULED` devices outnumber the expected concurrent upgrade capacity. No suppression needed — alert if scheduled devices remain stalled beyond the expected completion window.

**Device rebooting after firmware activation appearing as temporarily offline.** After firmware activation, devices reboot and are briefly unreachable. The progress tracker may show a gap between activation and verification. Distinguish by checking whether the device returns to a healthy state within 15-30 minutes after activation. Suppress by allowing a 30-minute post-activation window before flagging the device as failed.

**Partial upgrade failure where image download succeeds but activation fails.** A device may successfully download the new firmware but fail during activation (incompatible configuration, insufficient resources). The progress tracker shows partial completion. Distinguish by checking the specific failure stage in the SWIM task status. Do not suppress — this requires manual investigation of the activation failure.

references.title: Catalyst Center SWIM Compliance API — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **swim** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`.
• Active SWIM upgrade campaign in Catalyst Center (devices in SCHEDULED, IN_PROGRESS, or COMPLETED status).
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
SWIM upgrade progress is tracked through the same SWIM API endpoint polled by the TA. Events include the upgrade status for each device in the campaign.

Key fields:
• `upgradeStatus`: "SCHEDULED", "IN_PROGRESS", "SUCCESS", "FAILURE", "NOT_STARTED".
• `deviceName`: device hostname.
• `taskId`: SWIM task identifier linking related upgrade operations.
• `distributionStatus`: image distribution progress.
• `activationStatus`: firmware activation progress.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim"
| stats latest(upgradeStatus) as status latest(distributionStatus) as dist_status latest(activationStatus) as act_status by deviceName, deviceFamily, taskId
| stats count as total dc(eval(if(status="SUCCESS",deviceName,null()))) as completed dc(eval(if(status="IN_PROGRESS",deviceName,null()))) as in_progress dc(eval(if(status="FAILURE",deviceName,null()))) as failed dc(eval(if(status="SCHEDULED",deviceName,null()))) as scheduled by taskId
| eval progress_pct=round(completed/total*100,1)
| eval status_summary=completed." completed, ".in_progress." in progress, ".failed." failed, ".scheduled." scheduled"
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`stats latest(upgradeStatus) ... by deviceName, taskId`**: Gets the most recent status per device per upgrade task.
• **`dc(eval(if(status="SUCCESS",...)))`**: Counts devices in each status category using conditional distinct counts.
• **`progress_pct`**: Upgrade campaign progress as a percentage — the primary KPI for campaign tracking.
• **`by taskId`**: Groups by upgrade task, allowing tracking of multiple concurrent campaigns.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Design > Image Repository > Tasks. Compare the task progress with the Splunk results.
• **Device spot-check:** Pick a device in the campaign and verify its status matches between Catalyst Center and Splunk.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Progress bar showing campaign completion percentage, timechart of upgrade completions over time, and a table of failed devices requiring attention.
• **Alert:** Trigger when `failed > 0` — any upgrade failure needs investigation. Also alert when `progress_pct` has not increased in 4+ hours during an active campaign (possible stall).

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Progress appears to stall:** Catalyst Center has concurrent upgrade limits. Check whether `in_progress` is at the maximum concurrent limit while `scheduled` devices are waiting.
• **Failed devices:** Check the specific failure reason in Catalyst Center > Design > Image Repository > Tasks > [task] > [device]. Common failures: insufficient flash, incompatible firmware, device unreachable.
• **Campaign status inconsistent:** The TA polls at intervals; a device may complete its upgrade between polls, causing a brief status lag.
• **No SWIM events arriving:** SWIM requires a custom scripted input; verify the script is deployed and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages.
• **NULL or empty values in target version:** devices without a golden image assignment have no target; missing values indicate no SWIM policy is configured for that device family.
• **Fewer devices than expected:** check the service account role; credential access denied errors indicate insufficient permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We track the progress of software updates across your entire network, showing which devices have been updated, which are in progress, and which failed.
